-heterodimer

T-Cell Receptor

T-cell receptor is structurally similar to antibody proteins
Note:
-immunoglobulin like structure with Variable and Constant regions
-T cell receptor is monovalent, not divalent like antibody
Immunoglobulin superfamily
T-Cell receptors come in two types, ab, which is very abundant (90-99%) and gd

gd Receptors
-Precise function of is still unknown
-do not require antigen presentation
-May be more similar to pattern recognition receptors of the innate 	immune system
-Recognizes species specific antigens. In humans Mycoplasma 	tuberculosis and Hemophilius influenzae, malaria…

Va 79               Vd 6
So, T cell receptors are similar in structure to immunoglobulins:
a g is to light
b d is to heavy

3-D view of Light chain
Flattened view of Light chain
Sequence view of Highly variable part of Light chain
CDR- complementarity determining regions
(hypervarible)
Constant regions have exons grouped in functional domains
Note: T cell receptor generation is slightly different than immunoglobulins

*Note: this is first, just like heavy chain
*

TCR genes do not appear to undergo Somatic Hypermutation
Note: T cell receptor generation is slightly different than immunoglobulins

4 Distinct mechanisms contribute to the diversity of T cell receptors
1) multiple germline V, D, and J elements
2) combinatorial joining of the V, D, and J elements within a 			family
3) nibbling by exonuclease and or nucleotide addition by 			terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase during 			rearrangement
4) the ab or gd chains may be paired in different combinations

CD3 is a complex of 5 (gdezh)  invariant proteins that associate to form three dimers
